original_hypothesis.title: GaugeV2.setInternalBribe zero-address check is broken, enabling misconfiguration that permanently bricks fee forwarding
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeV2.setInternalBribe uses `require(_int >= address(0), 'ZA')`, which always passes. Owner (or an allowed admin through GaugeFactory batch ops) can set internal_bribe = address(0). Then GaugeV2.claimFees() will attempt safeApprove and call notifyRewardAmount on address(0), reverting and blocking fee distribution from gauge to bribes. This can silently accumulate fees without distribution, harming stakers and protocol operations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Phase A invariant INV-ACCESS-002 identifies the tautological check and bricking path. While privileged, it is a realistic operational foot-gun and can be exploited under compromised admin.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setInternalBribe(address _int) external onlyOwner { require(_int >= address(0), 'ZA'); internal_bribe = _int; }

preconditions: Caller has GaugeV2 owner privileges (direct owner or via batch admin actions elsewhere)
Gauge is for a Pair (isForPair == true) and claimFees is used operationally

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Zero-address validation is ineffective; downstream call path uses internal_bribe as an external contract

operational_questions: Are there safeguards/monitoring to prevent internal_bribe from being set to zero?
Who controls GaugeV2 ownership in production (multisig, timelock, EOA)?
Is claimFees called by anyone permissionlessly (it is), and will it repeatedly revert causing UX issues?
validation_requirements: Test setInternalBribe(address(0)) then call claimFees; confirm revert and fee forwarding failure
Inventory all gauges and current internal_bribe values
Review GaugeFactory batch ops that can set internal bribes at scale

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Gain owner-level access (outside threat model for HIGH realism) or exploit operator mistake
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Any claimFees attempt reverts; fees stop being distributed
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Exploit reputational/operational damage; optionally combine with other disruptions

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Fee bribe incentives stop; governance/voter incentives degraded
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low (fixable by admin) but damages accrue while broken
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Gauge fee harvesting and bribe funding

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Transactions setting internal_bribe to 0x0; repeated claimFees reverts
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Bribe contracts not receiving expected fee notifications
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Batch admin updates affecting many gauges simultaneously

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
